summary: Supreme Court Gutts Voting Rights Act, Threatens Black Political Power, New Grads Face Tough Labor Market as Black Joblessness Rises, NAACP and Truth Initiative Launch Health Equity Push on Tobacco, UNCF Launches Work-Integrated Learning Accelerator for HBCUs, 97 Rising Seniors Complete TMCF SOAR Program at Grambling State, Black Tech Week Returns to Cincinnati with National Founder Lineup, George E. Johnson, Johnson Products Founder, Remembered at 99, Joint Center Hosts Future of Black Communities Summit in DC

Freedom Summer 2026 mobilizes Black voters nationwide, 12-year-old Georgia twins launch profitable 3D-printing toy business, UNCF launches work-integrated learning accelerator for HBCUs, 97 rising seniors complete SOAR leadership program at Grambling, Black women's labor force participation continues to decline, Black founders raised $643M in Q1 2026, but disparity persists, George E. Johnson honored as Black business pioneer, Black Maternal Mental Health Week centers justice and community, NC A&T expects 200K visitors for Homecoming celebration

Supreme Court Ruling Threatens Local Reparations Programs, NC A&T Expects 200K Visitors for Centennial Homecoming, Twins Build $200/Month 3D-Printing Toy Business from $600 Loan, Black Unemployment Rate Masks Deeper Labor Market Crisis, Lauryn Hill Honored with Inaugural BET Awards Living Legend Award, Six HBCUs Launch Course-Sharing Partnership to Improve Graduation Rates, Where Is My Land Fights for Black Families' Property Rights, Black Tech Week Cincinnati Concludes with Cross-Sector Collaboration Focus, Study Reveals Medical Test Disparities for Black, Native American Women

Supreme Court Decimates Voting Rights Protections for Black Voters, HBCU Consortium Launches Course-Sharing to Improve Graduation Timelines, Black Tech Founders Secure $643M in Q1 2026—Strongest Quarter Since 2022, Truth Initiative and NAACP Launch Breath of Freedom Movement on Tobacco Harm, 2026 BET Awards Celebrate Generational Black Excellence and Lineage, Spelman Names Roboticist Ayanna Howard as 12th President, International Black Theatre Festival Returns July 27–August 1, Crenshaw and Slauson Intersection Renamed Nipsey Hussle Square

George E. Johnson, Black Business Pioneer, Dies at 99, Black Women Disappearing From Labor Force Despite Rate Drop, HBCU Enrollment Boom: Spelman Becomes Highly Selective, 2026 BET Awards Honor Black Excellence Across Generations
Supreme Court Protects Lisa Cook's Fed Role From Trump, HBCU Leaders Convene in Atlanta for UNITE 2026, Patti LaBelle Honored as Culture Icon at Essence Fest
